tool_result.description: Random/Multiple Cylinder Misfire Detected. The engine control module has detected misfires that are not isolated to a single cylinder, commonly caused by ignition, fuel delivery, or vacuum issues.
repair_steps: Inspect spark plugs and ignition coils for wear or damage
Check fuel pressure and fuel injector operation
Inspect intake system for vacuum leaks
Scan for cylinder-specific misfire codes to narrow the cause
Clear codes and test drive to confirm resolution

tool_result.description: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 1). The catalytic converter is not reducing emissions effectively, often due to converter degradation, an exhaust leak upstream, or a failing oxygen sensor.
repair_steps: Inspect catalytic converter for physical damage or contamination
Check upstream and downstream O2 sensors for correct operation
Verify there are no exhaust leaks before or after the catalyst
Clear the code and perform a drive cycle to confirm repair

tool_result.description: Heated Oxygen Sensor Control Circuit (Bank 1, Sensor 1). The oxygen sensor heater circuit is not functioning correctly, preventing the sensor from reaching operating temperature quickly.
repair_steps: Inspect oxygen sensor heater wiring and connector for damage or corrosion
Test oxygen sensor heater resistance against specifications
Check heater relay and fuse continuity
Replace oxygen sensor if heater element is faulty
Clear code and verify heater operation with infrared thermometer

tool_result.description: Injector Circuit Malfunction. The engine control module has detected a problem in the fuel injector circuit.
repair_steps: Check fuel injector wiring harness for damage or corrosion
Test injector coil resistance on all cylinders
Verify injector control signal from engine control module
Check fuel pressure supply to injectors
Test injectors individually for proper spray pattern
Replace faulty fuel injectors and clear code
